title: Plot Hole
wikitext:
[]: Plot Hole?             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Sep 04, 2006 11:42 pm Why are the Angels attacking Tokyo-3 when Adam is still in Germany or wherever. Why aren't they attacking where Adam is stored?

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Sep 04, 2006 11:46 pm They believe (mistakingly) that Lilith is Adam.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Mon Sep 04, 2006 11:50 pm Ah. But I thought they were attracted by instinct.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 05, 2006 12:03 am The matter, quite simply, can only be solved with some good fanwank. teh Classified Information wrote: 6. Special Agency Nerv D. In-Depth Information Nerv Headquarters was constructed inside the Black Moon containing Lilith. The Angels are all heading for Nerv H.Q. so that they can make contact with Lilith, the white giant in Terminal Dogma. They were aiming for her from the start, not Adam. 15. Angel D. In-Depth Information Two Seeds of Life cannot exist on one planet, and, therefore, one of them is excluded. As recorded in the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls, Adam-based life took part in a contest of survival, putting the stakes on their own existence. Some of them were trying to access Lilith and reset all life, some of them had nothing in mind, and some were trying to recover their progenitor Adam. The Angels -- Adam-based life -- became active under their respective tactics for survival and success.

[]:             Quote Post by Nukie » Thu Sep 14, 2006 7:13 pm YAY FOR CONFLICTING EVIDENCE! The CI was good for some things, but it totally made my brain explode. Mainly, the Lance. WTF?

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Thu Sep 14, 2006 7:21 pm It can just pick itself up and toss itself. It's THAT awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Sep 14, 2006 10:29 pm Mundane wrote: It can just pick itself up and toss itself. It's THAT awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Sep 15, 2006 10:27 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Afterall, it only had one of its legs chopped off below the knee. It's plausible.

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Sep 17, 2006 5:42 am But why can't two Seeds of Life exist on one planet? :?

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Sun Sep 17, 2006 6:44 am FauÃŸtin wrote: But why can't two Seeds of Life exist on one planet? :?

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Sun Sep 17, 2006 8:40 am But why do they need a separate ecosystem? Why couldn't they share it? :? Like Zeus, Poseidon and Hades shared the world with each other. Or they could have merged and played Darwin... Or they did started playing Darwin? Maybe... :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Legend of the Past » Mon Sep 18, 2006 12:14 am FauÃŸtin wrote: But why do they need a separate ecosystem? Why couldn't they share it? :? Like Zeus, Poseidon and Hades shared the world with each other. Or they could have merged and played Darwin... Or they did started playing Darwin? Maybe... :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by Faußtin » Mon Sep 18, 2006 12:42 pm Legend of the Past wrote: FauÃŸtin wrote: But why do they need a separate ecosystem? Why couldn't they share it? :? Like Zeus, Poseidon and Hades shared the world with each other. Or they could have merged and played Darwin... Or they did started playing Darwin? Maybe... :twisted:

The Angels are all heading for Nerv H.Q. so that they can make contact with Lilith, the white giant in Terminal Dogma. They were aiming for her from the start, not Adam. 15. Angel D. In-Depth Information Two Seeds of Life cannot exist on one planet, and, therefore, one of them is excluded. As recorded in the Secret Dead Sea Scrolls, Adam-based life took part in a contest of survival, putting the stakes on their own existence. Some of them were trying to access Lilith and reset all life, some of them had nothing in mind, and some were trying to recover their progenitor Adam.
